avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack remains generally unstable. Afternoon: Slight decrease in avalanche danger as the snowfall eases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Some snow will fall until midday. As a consequence of snowfall and the strong northwesterly wind, the snow drift accumulations will increase in size in the late morning.
Especially on very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes medium-sized and large natural dry avalanches are possible. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach very large size in some cases.
In the typical avalanche paths in these regions the avalanches can as before reach as far as the valley bottom.

The conditions are treacherous for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities outside marked and open pistes.
Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches, including large ones. Caution is to be exercised on steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 50 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1500 m. Over a wide area 5 to 15 cm of snow will fall on Wednesday above approximately 1300 m. Until Wednesday the wind will be strong over a wide area over a wide area.
The fresh snow as well as the sometimes large wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering.
The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Some large powder avalanches have been released as the day progresses. Several slab avalanches have been released in the last few days.

tendency.highlights: New snow and wind slabs: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and stormy weather a dangerous avalanche situation will persist in some regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall and the occasionally storm force northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last three days. This applies in particular in the regions exposed to the foehn wind and in areas close to the tree line, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
The avalanche-prone wind slabs can be released easily, even by a single winter sport participant,.

The peak of avalanche activity will be reached in the late morning in the regions exposed to the foehn wind. On wind-loaded slopes and in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place large and, in isolated cases, very large dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions over a wide area 40 to 80 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Monday.
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes. In particular at a distance from ridgelines,: In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes strong wind has transported the fresh and old snow significantly. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, snow drift accumulations will form by the early morning. These are to be found in particular between approximately 2100 and 2500 m.

In particular intermediate and high altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: Soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in areas close to the tree line.

In addition the hard wind slabs of the last few days in pass areas in all aspects and generally at elevated altitudes are capable of being triggered now as before. This applies in particular in case of a large load.

Hardly any small and medium-sized dry and moist avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

snowpackStructure.comment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, but less in some localities, has fallen since Monday above approximately 1700 m. The strong wind has transported a lot of snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Hardly any weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and east facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent. The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1700 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in some places be released easily. New snow and wind slabs require caution. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line as well as in areas close to the tree line. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach very large size in isolated cases. The avalanche prone locations are numerous difficult to recognise.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered and natural avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in particular on wind-protected shady slopes. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered. Medium-sized and large natural avalanches are possible on wind-loaded slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

bulletins.highlights: Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some regions.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released very easily. Fresh wind slabs are to be evaluated critically.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants. This applies on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.
Avalanches can reach large size in isolated cases. Caution is to be exercised in particular on north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Here such avalanche prone locations are more widespread.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.

In addition the fresh wind slabs of the last few days in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some locations. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found in areas close to the tree line. The prevalence of avalanche prone locations and likelihood of triggering will increase with altitude.

The avalanche situation is a little more favourable in highly frequented terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday. The wind was strong to storm force at times.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering. The new snow and wind slabs are bonding only slowly with the old snowpack. In some places various wind slab layers are lying on surface hoar. Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights:
Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

Thursday:
Up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be strong to storm force at times. The new snow and wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack in many places in all aspects.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The snowpack will be unstable over a wide area. The wind will be strong to storm force at times. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind the size of the avalanche prone locations will increase in the late morning.
Especially on very steep slopes and on wind-loaded slopes medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.
In some places the avalanches can be triggered in the old snow and reach quite a large size. In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can in isolated cases reach intermediate altitudes.
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches. Caution is to be exercised on very steep slopes, and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 40 cm of snow has fallen since Sunday above approximately 1600 m. Over a wide area 5 to 10 cm of snow will fall on Wednesday above approximately 1300 m. Until Wednesday the wind will be strong to storm force in some localities.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. The snowpack is unfavourably layered.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Several medium-sized to large natural dry avalanches have been released as the day progresses.

tendency.highlights: New snow and wind slabs: The avalanche danger will persist.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: At elevated altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of new snow and wind, wind slabs formed. They must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. They can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs remain prone to triggering in all aspects. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions gave rise to gradual consolidation of the snowpack in some places in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Heavy snowfall to low altitudes: The avalanche danger will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The wind was strong to storm force over a wide area. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The sometimes storm force wind has transported a lot of snow. As a consequence of the strong northwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed during the last few days. They are to be found in particular between approximately 1900 and 2300 m.
In addition the hard wind slabs of the last few days in pass areas in all aspects and generally at elevated altitudes are capable of being triggered now as before. This applies in particular in case of a large load.

In particular intermediate altitudes as well as in places that are protected from the wind: The soft wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The strong wind has transported a lot of snow.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

In particular at intermediate and high altitudes a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger. Fresh wind slabs require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released easily by individual winter sport participants especially in little used terrain. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Mostly avalanches are medium-sized.

In addition the fresh wind slabs of the last few days in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain are prone to triggering in some cases still. Such avalanche prone locations are to be found on north, east and southeast facing slopes above the tree line. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found in areas close to the tree line.

Restraint is advisable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions 10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen since Sunday. The wind was strong at times.

The snowpack will be prone to triggering. Wind slabs are lying on surface hoar in some places. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack. This applies in particular on west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line.

tendency.highlights:
Weak layers in the old snowpack represent the main danger.

Thursday:
10 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The wind will be strong to storm force at times. The new snow and wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack in many places in all aspects. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche danger will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs are to be evaluated with care and prudence. Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind was strong at times. Over a wide area precarious wind slabs formed. These are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1900 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.
Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. In some places the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm sign. The avalanche prone locations are quite prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. In particular on very steep shady slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large avalanches are possible.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended. Experience and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment: Above approximately 1900 m sometimes easily released wind slabs formed. Weak layers in the old snowpack are treacherous. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wind-loaded slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable. Avalanches can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large slab avalanches are possible. Mostly small natural avalanches are possible below approximately 1800 m.

snowpackStructure.comment: Below approximately 1800 m only a little snow is lying. In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. In the last few days sometimes deep wind slabs formed in particular in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In some places wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack represent the main danger. In addition the fresh wind slabs should be taken into account.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released by people. Mostly the avalanches are medium-sized but easily released. In some places the avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above the tree line, and on little used shady slopes. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye.
The wind was strong at times in some regions, especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line. The wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant. Caution is to be exercised at their margins in particular.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack remains generally prone to triggering. Distinct weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. Caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.
At high altitude the wind slabs have increased in size additionally in the last two days.

tendency.highlights: Some snow will fall on Thursday. The new snow and wind slabs are bonding poorly with the old snowpack in many places in all aspects. The avalanche danger will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. The wind slabs can be released in isolated cases in particular on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
The fresh snow and in particular the mostly small wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The snowpack will be moist at low altitude.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies especially on steep shady slopes adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line.
As a consequence of the strong northerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. These are in some cases prone to triggering. In some cases avalanches are medium-sized.

Backcountry touring calls for caution and restraint. Only a small amount of snow is lying for the time of year.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow has fallen. Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack. The fresh and older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

Low and intermediate altitudes: The snowpack is moist and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is barely capable of bearing a load.

avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche-prone wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Avalanches can be released, mostly by large additional loads.

snowpackStructure.comment: The fresh and older wind slabs remain prone to triggering. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes. The weather conditions gave rise to gradual consolidation of the snowpack in some cases.

tendency.highlights: Heavy snowfall to low altitudes: The avalanche danger will increase.
